arts.ttl: Chelsea willing to offer €35m for Tottenham-linked star, but Spurs have advantage in race
arts.sum: Chelsea have been linked with a move for the Atletico Madrid midfielder Johnny Cardoso at the end of the season. According to a report from Fichajes, they would be willing to offer €35 million in order to sign the 23-year-old United States international. The player has been heavily linked with a move to Tottenham Hotspur […] The post Chelsea willing to offer €35m for Tottenham-linked star, but Spurs have advantage in race appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Why isn't Wolves vs Tottenham live on TV in UK today?
arts.sum: Spurs are looking for the perfect result ahead of their huge Europa League showdown

arts.ttl: What Tottenham's new CEO will bring from Arsenal and how it will affect Daniel Levy
arts.sum: Other clubs, both in the Premier League and abroad, had been eyeing up Venkatesham but Tottenham moved quickly to create the new role in N17 for a man well known to chairman Daniel Levy . The club announced on Friday that the 44-year-old former Arsenal supremo would be arriving in the months after the end of this current season to bring his experience across north London to the team in white after a year out of the game deciding on his next move.

arts.ttl: Wolves vs Tottenham: Preview, predictions and lineups

arts.ttl: Spurs star Destiny Udogie makes Europa League admission - 'We all want to win it'
arts.sum: Spurs travel to Wolves in the Premier League on Sunday but will inevitably have one eye on next week's Europa League clash with Eintracht Frankfurt in Germany

arts.ttl: Man United's £62m target Matheus Cunha hits out at Wolves fan channel over deleted post on Instagram which declared he would 'follow his dreams'
arts.sum: Cunha has been in red-hot form this season, scoring 15 goals in 29 games across all competitions, and he is all but certain to move on this summer.

arts.ttl: Pereira flourishes at Wolves until the boom-bust cycle repeats itself | Jonathan Wilson
arts.sum: Manager’s predecessor, Gary O’Neil, also enjoyed a fine start before things took a seemingly inevitable downturn Managers rise and managers fall and often there isn’t much reason for it. It was only a year ago that Gary O’Neil seemed one of the brightest young managers in the Premier League, but by December it was over. This is how football is: when a blip becomes a slump becomes a spiral, the only solution is the sacrifice of the manager. It often works: Wolves have improved dramatically under Vítor Pereira and, while they may not yet be mathematically safe from relegation, they surely soon will be. The life of man, the folk carol reminds us, is but a span; the life of a manager is even shorter (but a spanager?). O’Neil had replaced Scott Parker at Bournemouth four games into 2022-23, after their 9-0 defeat by Liverpool, and had kept them up comfortably, only to be jettisoned for Andoni Iraola. He took over Wolves less than a week before last season began and had them in the top half in March. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Matheus Cunha clashes with Wolves fans after deleting eye-catching statement
arts.sum: Matheus Cunha deleted a post on Instagram - but not before it had been screenshotted and widely shared among unhappy Wolves fans, sparking an argument on social media
